trans-unit.source: Exercise: OpenStreetMap data export
trans-unit.target: Exercise: OpenStreetMap data export

trans-unit.source: This exercise aims to show two ways how to get OpenStreetMap(OSM) as a vector file into QGIS. We will go through the workflow using Geofabrik, the HOT (Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team) export tool and the QuickOSM QGIS-plugin.
trans-unit.target: This exercise aims to show two ways how to get OpenStreetMap(OSM) as a vector file into QGIS. We will go through the workflow using Geofabrik, the HOT (Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team) export tool and the QuickOSM QGIS-plugin.

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: QGIS Essentials
trans-unit.target: QGIS Essentials

trans-unit.source: Finding and downloading relevant datasets and preparing them for further analysis
trans-unit.target: Finding and downloading relevant datasets and preparing them for further analysis

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: The exercise takes around 2 hours to complete, depending on the number of participants and their familiarity with computer systems.
trans-unit.target: The exercise takes around 2 hours to complete, depending on the number of participants and their familiarity with computer systems.

trans-unit.source: [QGIS Interface](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [QGIS Interface](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Types of Geodata](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geodata_types_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Types of Geodata](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geodata_types_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Layer Concept](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Layer Concept](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Classification - Graduated](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_graduated_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Classification - Graduated](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_graduated_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.source: Since the exercise is about finding data, there won't be any data to download. Instead download the __standard folder structure__ [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_2/Exercise_3/Module_2_Exercise_3_Data_sources.zip) and insert your data as you download it.
trans-unit.target: Since the exercise is about finding data, there won't be any data to download. Instead download the __standard folder structure__ [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_2/Exercise_3/Module_2_Exercise_3_Data_sources.zip) and insert your data as you download it.

trans-unit.source: Tasks
trans-unit.target: Tasks

trans-unit.source: OpenStreetMap (OSM) is a collaborative, open-source project that creates free, editable maps of the world, built by a global community of mappers. There are multiple different ways how to download or export data from OpenStreetMap (OSM), each with it's own advantage. In this exercise, we will go over a few of these methods and discuss their advantages.
trans-unit.target: OpenStreetMap (OSM) is a collaborative, open-source project that creates free, editable maps of the world, built by a global community of mappers. There are multiple different ways how to download or export data from OpenStreetMap (OSM), each with it's own advantage. In this exercise, we will go over a few of these methods and discuss their advantages.

trans-unit.source: Task 1: Geofabrik
trans-unit.target: Task 1: Geofabrik

trans-unit.source: The Geofabrik website offers downloads of OSM data by region.
trans-unit.target: The Geofabrik website offers downloads of OSM data by region.

trans-unit.source: Go to __https://download.geofabrik.de/__ and navigate to the Mauritius dataset by clicking on `Africa` → ` Mauritius`
trans-unit.target: Go to __https://download.geofabrik.de/__ and navigate to the Mauritius dataset by clicking on `Africa` → ` Mauritius`

trans-unit.source: Under __Commonly Used Formats__ select the option `mauritius-latest-free.shp.zip` and download the file. Place it somewhere on your computer where you can find it again and unzip it.
trans-unit.target: Under __Commonly Used Formats__ select the option `mauritius-latest-free.shp.zip` and download the file. Place it somewhere on your computer where you can find it again and unzip it.

trans-unit.source: Check out the content of the folder. There are many different shapefiles, each containing one kind of OSM data. The whole list of layers and what they include can be found [here](https://download.geofabrik.de/osm-data-in-gis-formats-free.pdf).
trans-unit.target: Check out the content of the folder. There are many different shapefiles, each containing one kind of OSM data. The whole list of layers and what they include can be found [here](https://download.geofabrik.de/osm-data-in-gis-formats-free.pdf).

trans-unit.source: Open a new QGIS project and save it in the `/Project` folder of your exercise folder.
trans-unit.target: Open a new QGIS project and save it in the `/Project` folder of your exercise folder.

trans-unit.source: Load the file `gis_osm_places_a_free_1.shp`. This polygon layer contains the boundaries of different features. Take some time to explore the data by using the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Load the file `gis_osm_places_a_free_1.shp`. This polygon layer contains the boundaries of different features. Take some time to explore the data by using the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: (Optional) You can select all features within the layer with the “fclass” = “island” and export them as a new layer. This will allow you to start creating a map of the islands of Mauritius.
trans-unit.target: (Optional) You can select all features within the layer with the “fclass” = “island” and export them as a new layer. This will allow you to start creating a map of the islands of Mauritius.

trans-unit.source: Load the file `gis_osm_buildings_a_free_1.shp`. This polygon layer contains all buildings in Mauritius mapped on OSM. Take some time to explore the layer.
trans-unit.target: Load the file `gis_osm_buildings_a_free_1.shp`. This polygon layer contains all buildings in Mauritius mapped on OSM. Take some time to explore the layer.

trans-unit.source: Add a satellite base map by using the [QuickMapServices plugin](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_basemaps_wiki.html#basemaps-from-quickmapservices-plugin) to check if there are unmapped buildings.
trans-unit.target: Add a satellite base map by using the [QuickMapServices plugin](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_basemaps_wiki.html#basemaps-from-quickmapservices-plugin) to check if there are unmapped buildings.

trans-unit.source: Load the file `gis_osm_landuse_a_free_1.shp`. Check out the dataset and use the classification function to get a better overview.
trans-unit.target: Load the file `gis_osm_landuse_a_free_1.shp`. Check out the dataset and use the classification function to get a better overview.

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer `gis_osm_landuse_a_free_1` in the `Layer Panel` → `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer `gis_osm_landuse_a_free_1` in the `Layer Panel` → `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab.

trans-unit.source: On the top you find a dropdown menu. Open it and choose `Categorized`. Under `Value` select “fclass” (short for _featureclass_).
trans-unit.target: On the top you find a dropdown menu. Open it and choose `Categorized`. Under `Value` select “fclass” (short for _featureclass_).

trans-unit.source: Further down the window click on `Classify`. You will see all unique values of the “fclass” column. You can adjust the colours by double-clicking on one row in the central field. Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.
trans-unit.target: Further down the window click on `Classify`. You will see all unique values of the “fclass” column. You can adjust the colours by double-clicking on one row in the central field. Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.

trans-unit.source: As you can see, Geofabrik is great if you want to get complete OSM datasets for whole countries or regions.
trans-unit.target: As you can see, Geofabrik is great if you want to get complete OSM datasets for whole countries or regions.

trans-unit.source: Advantages
trans-unit.target: Advantages

trans-unit.source: Disadvantages
trans-unit.target: Disadvantages

trans-unit.source: + Quick access to complete OSM datasets
trans-unit.target: + Quick access to complete OSM datasets

trans-unit.source: - If one is only interested in specific features or regions (other then countries), not optimal
trans-unit.target: - If one is only interested in specific features or regions (other then countries), not optimal

trans-unit.source: + Very up-to-date OSM exports
trans-unit.target: + Very up-to-date OSM exports

trans-unit.source: - Large file size
trans-unit.target: - Large file size

trans-unit.source: + Clear documentation of which OSM features are contained in each shapefile
trans-unit.target: + Clear documentation of which OSM features are contained in each shapefile

trans-unit.source: - Only available as shapefile
trans-unit.target: - Only available as shapefile

trans-unit.source: Task 2: HOT Export Tool
trans-unit.target: Task 2: HOT Export Tool

trans-unit.source: The [HOT Export Tool](https://export.hotosm.org/v3/) is a tool for accessing OSM data offered by Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT). HOT offers a browser-based tool to download OSM data with good options to specify region, time, feature type and data format.
trans-unit.target: The [HOT Export Tool](https://export.hotosm.org/v3/) is a tool for accessing OSM data offered by Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT). HOT offers a browser-based tool to download OSM data with good options to specify region, time, feature type and data format.

trans-unit.source: Go to the HOT Export tool. To use the tool you need an OSM account. If you don't have one you need to create one. Click on `Log in`. In the new window select the option to create a new account.
trans-unit.target: Go to the HOT Export tool. To use the tool you need an OSM account. If you don't have one you need to create one. Click on `Log in`. In the new window select the option to create a new account.

trans-unit.source: If you have an OSM account you can log in directly into the HOT Export tool by clicking on `Log in`.
trans-unit.target: If you have an OSM account you can log in directly into the HOT Export tool by clicking on `Log in`.

trans-unit.source: In this example, we want to download all banking and finance features from OSM in Mauritius.
trans-unit.target: In this example, we want to download all banking and finance features from OSM in Mauritius.

trans-unit.source: Select area or location: Zoom to Mauritius on the map or use the search bar. To mark the main island there are three options. You can draw a box, draw a polygon or upload a GeoJSON file with your boundaries. In this case, use one of the first two options to mark Mauritius.
trans-unit.target: Select area or location: Zoom to Mauritius on the map or use the search bar. To mark the main island there are three options. You can draw a box, draw a polygon or upload a GeoJSON file with your boundaries. In this case, use one of the first two options to mark Mauritius.

trans-unit.source: Name and description: give your export the name “Mauritius financial institutions” and add a short description of your export.
trans-unit.target: Name and description: give your export the name “Mauritius financial institutions” and add a short description of your export.

trans-unit.source: Format tab: The HOT Export tool offers many data formats in which you can export data. Select GeoPackage (`.gpkg`) and leave the other options unchecked.
trans-unit.target: Format tab: The HOT Export tool offers many data formats in which you can export data. Select GeoPackage (`.gpkg`) and leave the other options unchecked.

trans-unit.source: Data tab: The easiest way to select the data you want to download is the Tag Tree. Since we want to download financial institutions, find the “Financial” category and select all options (ATM, Bank, Bureau de Change).
trans-unit.target: Data tab: The easiest way to select the data you want to download is the Tag Tree. Since we want to download financial institutions, find the “Financial” category and select all options (ATM, Bank, Bureau de Change).

trans-unit.source: Summary tab: Click on `Create Export`. You will be forwarded to a page to wait until the export is finished. When the processing is finished, the page will show a download link for your file.
trans-unit.target: Summary tab: Click on `Create Export`. You will be forwarded to a page to wait until the export is finished. When the processing is finished, the page will show a download link for your file.

trans-unit.source: Load the new file in QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Load the new file in QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Arrange the layers on the map so you can see the new layer.
trans-unit.target: Arrange the layers on the map so you can see the new layer.

trans-unit.source: (optional) Use the classification function to get a better overview to get a better overview:
trans-unit.target: (optional) Use the classification function to get a better overview to get a better overview:

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer `Mauritius_finical_institution` in the `Layer Panel` → `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer `Mauritius_finical_institution` in the `Layer Panel` → `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab.

trans-unit.source: On the top you find a dropdown menu. Open it and choose `Categorized`. Under `Value` select "amenity".
trans-unit.target: On the top you find a dropdown menu. Open it and choose `Categorized`. Under `Value` select "amenity".

trans-unit.source: Further down the window click on `Classify`. Now you should see all unique values or attributes of the selected “fclass” column. You can adjust the colours by double-clicking on one row in the central field. Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.
trans-unit.target: Further down the window click on `Classify`. Now you should see all unique values or attributes of the selected “fclass” column. You can adjust the colours by double-clicking on one row in the central field. Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.

trans-unit.source: As you can see, the HOT Export tool offers a good mix of flexibility and quick access to OSM data. However, there are quite some steps involved until the data is in QGIS.
trans-unit.target: As you can see, the HOT Export tool offers a good mix of flexibility and quick access to OSM data. However, there are quite some steps involved until the data is in QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Advantages
trans-unit.target: Advantages

trans-unit.source: Disadvantages
trans-unit.target: Disadvantages

trans-unit.source: + Good options for data selection
trans-unit.target: + Good options for data selection

trans-unit.source: - Many steps involved
trans-unit.target: - Many steps involved

trans-unit.source: + Many different data formats available
trans-unit.target: + Many different data formats available

trans-unit.source: - Only fixed option for data selection
trans-unit.target: - Only fixed option for data selection

trans-unit.source: + Easy to use
trans-unit.target: + Easy to use

trans-unit.source: + Query can easily be repeated
trans-unit.target: + Query can easily be repeated

trans-unit.source: Task 3: QuickOSM
trans-unit.target: Task 3: QuickOSM

trans-unit.source: The QuickOSM plugin allows you to load OSM data directly into QGIS. However, the plugin requires the deepest knowledge of the OSM data model, compared to the previous two options. To tailor your query based on the exact key and value you need there are two great resources:
trans-unit.target: The QuickOSM plugin allows you to load OSM data directly into QGIS. However, the plugin requires the deepest knowledge of the OSM data model, compared to the previous two options. To tailor your query based on the exact key and value you need there are two great resources:

trans-unit.source: [OSM Wiki](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Main_Page), and especially the [Map features](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Map_features) article.
trans-unit.target: [OSM Wiki](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Main_Page), and especially the [Map features](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Map_features) article.

trans-unit.source: [Taginfo](https://taginfo.openstreetmap.org/)
trans-unit.target: [Taginfo](https://taginfo.openstreetmap.org/)

trans-unit.source: Have a look at both.
trans-unit.target: Have a look at both.

trans-unit.source: Install the QuickOSM plugin by clicking on the `Plugin` tab, → `Manage and Install Plugins…` → `All` → Search for "QuickOSM" → `Install Plugin`.
trans-unit.target: Install the QuickOSM plugin by clicking on the `Plugin` tab, → `Manage and Install Plugins…` → `All` → Search for "QuickOSM" → `Install Plugin`.

trans-unit.source: Now we want to find all health facilities on the island of Mauritius.
trans-unit.target: Now we want to find all health facilities on the island of Mauritius.

trans-unit.source: Position the island of Mauritius in a way that the island is completely visible in your map canvas.
trans-unit.target: Position the island of Mauritius in a way that the island is completely visible in your map canvas.

trans-unit.source: Open the QuickOSM plugin by clicking on the `Vector` menu → `QuickOSM` → `QuickOSM`.
trans-unit.target: Open the QuickOSM plugin by clicking on the `Vector` menu → `QuickOSM` → `QuickOSM`.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Quick query`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Quick query`.

trans-unit.source: In the table, add "amenity" as the key and "hospital" as the value. This query will return hospital data.
trans-unit.target: In the table, add "amenity" as the key and "hospital" as the value. This query will return hospital data.

trans-unit.source: Click on the green plus icon to add another line to the table. In this line select “OR” in the small dropdown menu on the left-hand side of the new line.
trans-unit.target: Click on the green plus icon to add another line to the table. In this line select “OR” in the small dropdown menu on the left-hand side of the new line.

trans-unit.source: Add "healthcare" as a new key with "hospital" as the value.
trans-unit.target: Add "healthcare" as a new key with "hospital" as the value.

trans-unit.source: Below the table set the small dropdown menu to “Canvas Extent”
trans-unit.target: Below the table set the small dropdown menu to “Canvas Extent”

trans-unit.source: Click on `Run query`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Run query`.

trans-unit.source: Check out the new layers in the layer panel. Open the attribute table of the point layer. Check the “healthcare” and “amenity” columns. Which data would be missing if you would have used only one of the keys?
trans-unit.target: Check out the new layers in the layer panel. Open the attribute table of the point layer. Check the “healthcare” and “amenity” columns. Which data would be missing if you would have used only one of the keys?

trans-unit.source: Do the same query for La Reunion which is to the southwest of Mauritius. Move the island in the middle of your map canvas and click `Run query`. Does the attribute table of this new point layer look different?
trans-unit.target: Do the same query for La Reunion which is to the southwest of Mauritius. Move the island in the middle of your map canvas and click `Run query`. Does the attribute table of this new point layer look different?

trans-unit.source: What if we only want hospitals with an emergency room? In this case, we would need to build a query using a combination of the operators "OR" and "AND". Look at the image below.
trans-unit.target: What if we only want hospitals with an emergency room? In this case, we would need to build a query using a combination of the operators "OR" and "AND". Look at the image below.

trans-unit.source: Advantages
trans-unit.target: Advantages

trans-unit.source: Disadvantages
trans-unit.target: Disadvantages

trans-unit.source: + Query can be tailored for very specific data
trans-unit.target: + Query can be tailored for very specific data

trans-unit.source: - Requires knowledge of OSM data model
trans-unit.target: - Requires knowledge of OSM data model

trans-unit.source: + Data loads directly in QGIS
trans-unit.target: + Data loads directly in QGIS

trans-unit.source: - Building queries can quickly become complex
trans-unit.target: - Building queries can quickly become complex

trans-unit.source: + Query can easily be repeated
trans-unit.target: + Query can easily be repeated
